title: Whose land is the feed? How techno-feudalism explains Meta's censorship on queer and pro-choice
content: The platforms—Amazon, Google, Apple, Meta—are no longer ordinary firms competing in a market; they are fiefs. The way out is not a kinder lord; it is to stop being a tenant.
snippet: The platforms—Amazon, Google, Apple, Meta—are no longer ordinary firms competing in a market; they are fiefs. The way out is not a kinder lord; it is to stop being a tenant.
creator: info@waag.org (Waag)

title: NodeNet: mesh as local emergency network?
content: In collaboration with the municipality of Amsterdam and a group of residents from the Nieuwmarkt neighbourhood, Waag investigated how a decentralised radio network can contribute to a resilient communications infrastructure.

title: Rerooting
content: Join us at Waag on September 3 for the Det Digit Art House show & tell. Discover the works Anna Musikhina and Werner van der Zwan created during their residency, at the intersection of technology and ecology.

title: Waag’s Head of ICT on making choices for your organisation
content: For over 30 years, Waag has been working on open, fair and inclusive technology. Our research, our projects and our events: everything is centred around this mission. This also influences the choices you make internally as an organisation. How do you put that mission into practice?

title: How would you like to experience your library online?
content: The library is more than a place for books.  Join our workshop on Tuesday 21 July and share your ideas about the future of the library online! We want to explore how libraries can create meaningful online spaces for connection, knowledge sharing and exchange.

title: Bridging the gap: digital technology as enhancement, not replacement
content: How can digital technology support craftsmanship without removing the maker from the process? In this lecture, Aslı Aydın Aksan explores the role of digital technology within craft.
snippet: How can digital technology support craftsmanship without removing the maker from the process? In this lecture, Aslı Aydın Aksan explores the role of digital technology within craft.

title: GLUE 2026
content: During GLUE 2026, Waag Futurelab is hosting 6 artists and designers, showcasing how design can scratch at the surface of larger themes. Our designed world is broken, can we fix it? At least we can mend, patch, hack and repair.

title: You're banned by big tech. Now what?
content: Has your social media account been banned? You’re not alone. Here are five things you can do. Or download, fold, and spread the word!
snippet: Has your social media account been banned? You’re not alone. Here are five things you can do. Or download, fold, and spread the word!
creator: info@waag.org (Waag)

title: What's (More-than-)Human, Anyway?
content: We have long told a particular story about what it means to be human: a rational, autonomous individual, separate from nature, in control of the world it designs. This figure sits at the centre of meaning, politics, and technology as the measure of all things. But this story has a problem: the world it imagines - separating nature and culture, bodies and machines, self and environment - was never real, and now increasingly so.

creator: info@waag.org (Waag)

title: European digital ID wallets are a gift to Google and Apple
content: European governments are rolling out digital identity wallets, which are to be used by citizens to access services, and to verify their age online. There is a serious problem with this: these wallets rely on safety services of Google and Apple.

title: Help map AI-skills needs across Europe
content: The RenAIssance project is building a European collaborative network to ensure that AI reinforces our values rather than eroding them. Please complete the survey so that we can gain a better understanding of the needs in this area.

title: How does the municipality use algorithms? Help make this more transparent!
content: Algorithms affect benefits, taxes, and city services. Join our workshop to share your ideas and help improve transparency and data rights.

title: More grip on technology with the Public Stack Academy
content: Every decision made in the design and implementation of technology affects you, your organisation and our society. In the Public Stack Academy by Waag you will learn how to make impactful choices that align with your values.

title: Amsterdam, be explicit about technology
content: On Wednesday 3 June, PRO Amsterdam and D66 presented their coalition agreement. The plan does not address how the city can achieve digital independence. Together with Commons Network and Cybersoek, Waag is calling on the city council to be explicit about technology.

title: How to be close to the sea?
